mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.5%, corporate earnings growth decelerates sharply due to weak consumer spending and excess capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 7.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.03 during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which accelerates corporate revenue and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, with interaction term coefficients statistically indistinguishable from zero in a 3-year rolling OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during expansion phases, with a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly translates to higher corporate profit margins and revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of the business cycle phase.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no significant predictive relationship with 20-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, inflation expectations rise and force tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, or the threshold effect vanishes with a Rank IC < 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal underperforms single-series models, with interaction effects failing to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample forecasting.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in retail sales signal immediate consumer demand contraction, which triggers short-term negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during late-cycle economic phases.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction with a positive Rank IC > 0.03 in walk-forward validation.
